patterns.name: safety_immediate_response
patterns.when_to_use: When response must be instantaneous. No delay allowed between trigger and response.
decision_rules: Keywords: immediately, when, must, stop, abort, pause
patterns.text: Pattern: safety_immediate_response. Immediate response to dangerous conditions. Template: A[] (trigger_condition imply safety_action). Use when response must be instantaneous. No delay allowed between trigger and response. Keywords: immediately, when, must, stop, abort, pause.

patterns.name: sequential_workflow
patterns.when_to_use: For multi-step processes where one step leads to another with possible delays.
decision_rules: Keywords: after, once, then, eventually, follows
patterns.text: Pattern: sequential_workflow. One action eventually follows another. Template: action1 --> action2. Use for multi-step processes where one step leads to another with possible delays. Keywords: after, once, then, eventually, follows.

patterns.name: eventual_completion
patterns.when_to_use: For liveness properties where something must eventually happen.
decision_rules: Keywords: eventually, will, must complete, finish
patterns.text: Pattern: eventual_completion. Task must eventually complete. Template: A<> final_state. Use for liveness properties where something must eventually happen. Keywords: eventually, will, must complete, finish.

patterns.name: existential_reachability
patterns.description: Check if state is possible to reach (there exists a path)
patterns.when_to_use: To verify that something CAN happen or is POSSIBLE. Checks if at least one execution path exists.
decision_rules: Keywords: possible, can, might, able to, feasible, exists a way, could reach
patterns.text: Pattern: existential_reachability. Check if state is possible to reach. Template: E<> target_state. Use to verify that something CAN happen or is POSSIBLE. Keywords: possible, can, might, able to, feasible.

patterns.name: universal_reachability
patterns.description: Check if state is guaranteed reachable from any state (all paths can reach)
patterns.when_to_use: To verify that from ANY state, it's always POSSIBLE to eventually reach target.
decision_rules: Keywords: all paths, guaranteed reachable, always possible, every path allows, must be able to reach
patterns.text: Pattern: universal_reachability. State guaranteed reachable from any path. Template: A<> target_state. Use to verify from ANY state it is always possible to eventually reach the target. Keywords: all paths, guaranteed, always possible.

patterns.name: forbidden_state
patterns.when_to_use: To prevent dangerous or invalid states from ever happening.
decision_rules: Keywords: never, cannot, must not, prohibited
patterns.text: Pattern: forbidden_state. State must never occur. Template: A[] not (bad_state). Use to prevent dangerous or invalid states. Keywords: never, cannot, must not, prohibited.

patterns.name: conditional_response
patterns.when_to_use: For triggered behaviors where a condition leads to eventual action.
decision_rules: Keywords: if, when, causes, triggers, leads to
patterns.text: Pattern: conditional_response. If condition then eventual response. Template: condition --> response. Use for triggered behaviors where condition leads to eventual action. Keywords: if, when, causes, triggers, leads to.

patterns.name: time_bounded_constraint
patterns.description: Action must occur within specific time duration
patterns.when_to_use: When there is a maximum time allowed in a state or for a response.
decision_rules: Keywords: within X time, before X time, timeout, deadline, seconds, minutes
patterns.text: Pattern: time_bounded_constraint. Action must occur within specific time duration. Template: A[] (state imply clock <= T). Use when there is a maximum time allowed. Keywords: within, before, timeout, deadline, seconds, minutes.

operators.name: A[]
operators.description: Always - property holds in ALL reachable states at ALL times
operators.when_to_use: For safety properties. Things that must ALWAYS be true or NEVER happen. Use with imply for immediate responses.
operators.when_not_to_use: Don't use for eventual actions or when delay is acceptable.
operators.text: Operator: A[] (Always). Property holds in ALL reachable states at ALL times. Use for safety properties, invariants, forbidden states. Use with imply for immediate responses. Do not use for eventual actions.

operators.name: E<>
operators.description: Possibly - there exists at least one path where property eventually holds
operators.when_to_use: To check if something CAN happen or is POSSIBLE.
operators.when_not_to_use: Don't use when something MUST happen (use A<> instead).
operators.text: Operator: E<> (Possibly). There exists at least one path where property eventually holds. Use to check if something CAN happen. Keywords: possible, can, might. Do not use when something MUST happen.

operators.name: A<>
operators.description: Eventually - property eventually holds on ALL execution paths
operators.when_to_use: For liveness properties. Things that MUST eventually happen.
operators.when_not_to_use: Don't use for conditional responses (use --> instead).
operators.text: Operator: A<> (Eventually). Property eventually holds on ALL execution paths. Use for liveness - things that MUST eventually happen. Do not use for conditional responses.

operators.description: Leads-to - if left becomes true, right will eventually become true
operators.when_to_use: For sequential workflows and conditional eventual responses.
operators.when_not_to_use: Don't use for immediate responses (use A[] imply instead).
operators.text: Operator: --> (Leads-to). If left becomes true, right will eventually become true. Use for sequential workflows and conditional eventual responses. Do not use for immediate responses, use A[] imply instead.

operators.name: imply
operators.description: Logical implication in the SAME state (synchronous)
operators.when_to_use: For immediate responses. If condition is true, consequence must be true RIGHT NOW. Always used inside A[].
operators.when_not_to_use: Don't use for eventual responses (use --> instead).
operators.text: Operator: imply (immediate logical implication). Condition and consequence must hold in the SAME state. Always used inside A[]. Use for immediate safety responses. Do not use for eventual responses.

examples.nl: Drone must return to base when battery is below 10%
examples.text: Example: Drone must return to base when battery is below 10%. UPPAAL: A[] (Drone.battery < 10 imply Drone.returning_to_base). Pattern: safety_immediate_response. Uses A[] with imply for immediate safety response.

examples.nl: Robot must stop immediately when obstacle is detected
examples.text: Example: Robot must stop immediately when obstacle is detected. UPPAAL: A[] (Robot.obstacle_detected imply Robot.stopped). Pattern: safety_immediate_response.

examples.nl: Once scanning is done, UGV will eventually collect the container
examples.text: Example: Once scanning is done, UGV will eventually collect the container. UPPAAL: Drone.scanning_done --> UGV.collecting. Pattern: sequential_workflow. Uses --> for sequential trigger.

examples.nl: Both drones will eventually complete their missions
examples.text: Example: Both drones will eventually complete their missions. UPPAAL: A<> (DroneA.mission_complete && DroneB.mission_complete). Pattern: eventual_completion. Uses A<> for liveness.

examples.nl: It's possible for both robots to reach the charging station
examples.text: Example: It is possible for both robots to reach the charging station. UPPAAL: E<> (RobotA.at_charging_station && RobotB.at_charging_station). Pattern: existential_reachability.

examples.nl: System never allows operation with high vibration
examples.text: Example: System never allows operation with high vibration. UPPAAL: A[] not (vibration > 5.0 && System.operating). Pattern: forbidden_state. Uses A[] not to forbid dangerous state.

examples.nl: Visibility below 30% causes both drones to surface
examples.text: Example: Visibility below 30% causes both drones to surface. UPPAAL: (visibility < 30) --> (DroneA.at_surface && DroneB.at_surface). Pattern: conditional_response.

examples.nl: PickerBot aborts picking when load exceeds limit
examples.text: Example: PickerBot aborts picking when load exceeds limit. UPPAAL: A[] (PickerBot.load > limit imply not PickerBot.picking). Pattern: safety_immediate_response.

examples.nl: After inspection finishes, welder starts welding
examples.text: Example: After inspection finishes, welder starts welding. UPPAAL: InspectorDrone.inspection_complete --> Welder.welding. Pattern: sequential_workflow.

examples.nl: Robot cannot move while charging
examples.text: Example: Robot cannot move while charging. UPPAAL: A[] not (Robot.charging && Robot.moving). Pattern: forbidden_state.

examples.nl: System eventually reaches stable state
examples.text: Example: System eventually reaches stable state. UPPAAL: A<> System.stable. Pattern: eventual_completion.

examples.nl: When battery is low, robot goes to charging station
examples.text: Example: When battery is low, robot goes to charging station. UPPAAL: (Robot.battery < 20) --> Robot.at_charging_station. Pattern: conditional_response.

examples.nl: Welder pauses when temperature exceeds 100 degrees
examples.text: Example: Welder pauses when temperature exceeds 100 degrees. UPPAAL: A[] (Welder.temperature > 100 imply Welder.paused). Pattern: safety_immediate_response.

examples.nl: Can the drone reach the target location
examples.text: Example: Can the drone reach the target location. UPPAAL: E<> Drone.at_target. Pattern: existential_reachability.

examples.nl: System never deadlocks
examples.text: Example: System never deadlocks. UPPAAL: A[] not deadlock. Pattern: forbidden_state.

examples.nl: All inspection tasks will eventually be completed
examples.text: Example: All inspection tasks will eventually be completed. UPPAAL: A<> InspectionBot.all_tasks_done. Pattern: eventual_completion.

examples.nl: Robot will eventually return to base
examples.text: Example: Robot will eventually return to base. UPPAAL: A<> Robot.at_base. Pattern: eventual_completion.

examples.nl: Two robots cannot occupy same position simultaneously
examples.text: Example: Two robots cannot occupy same position simultaneously. UPPAAL: A[] not (RobotA.position == RobotB.position). Pattern: forbidden_state.

examples.nl: If error detected, system enters recovery mode
examples.text: Example: If error detected, system enters recovery mode. UPPAAL: System.error_detected --> System.recovery_mode. Pattern: conditional_response.

examples.nl: Charging completes then robot resumes operation
examples.text: Example: Charging completes then robot resumes operation. UPPAAL: Robot.charging_complete --> Robot.operating. Pattern: sequential_workflow.

examples.nl: Drone must stop after 10 minutes of flying
examples.text: Example: Drone must stop after 10 minutes of flying. UPPAAL: A[] (Drone.flying imply clock <= 10). Pattern: time_bounded_constraint. Clock enforces upper time bound.

examples.nl: Robot must reach base within 20 seconds when battery is low
examples.text: Example: Robot must reach base within 20 seconds when battery is low. UPPAAL: A[] (Robot.battery < 10 && not Robot.at_base imply clock <= 20). Pattern: time_bounded_constraint.

examples.nl: System must respond to alarm within 5 seconds
examples.text: Example: System must respond to alarm within 5 seconds. UPPAAL: A[] (System.alarm_active && not System.responded imply clock <= 5). Pattern: time_bounded_constraint.

examples.nl: Charging must complete within 30 minutes
examples.text: Example: Charging must complete within 30 minutes. UPPAAL: A[] (Robot.charging imply clock <= 30). Pattern: time_bounded_constraint.

examples.nl: Emergency stop must occur within 2 seconds of collision detection
examples.text: Example: Emergency stop must occur within 2 seconds of collision detection. UPPAAL: A[] (collision_detected && not emergency_stopped imply clock <= 2). Pattern: time_bounded_constraint.

examples.nl: All paths guarantee the robot can reach the safe zone
examples.text: Example: All paths guarantee the robot can reach the safe zone. UPPAAL: A<> Robot.at_safe_zone. Pattern: universal_reachability.

examples.nl: Every execution path allows the drone to return home
examples.text: Example: Every execution path allows the drone to return home. UPPAAL: A<> Drone.at_home. Pattern: universal_reachability.

examples.nl: System is guaranteed to be able to reach idle state
examples.text: Example: System is guaranteed to be able to reach idle state. UPPAAL: A<> System.idle. Pattern: universal_reachability.
